metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fantastic activities for your little one! Despite the rainy weather on Saturday, there are plenty of indoor options to keep them entertained, from puppet shows to museum adventures. Plus, there are exciting Easter-themed events coming up in the following weeks that you can book ahead for some springtime fun!

activities.title: Play Saturdays
activities.description: Drop-in play sessions at the Young V&A museum, offering interactive exhibits and activities designed for children. Explore the Imagine, Play and Design galleries.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits, interactive displays, and open studio space will engage a 3-year-old's curiosity and creativity. The museum is designed with children in mind, offering plenty of space to move and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk to the museum.
transport.tip: The museum is very close to the station, making it easily accessible.

activities.title: Make-Lab Masterclass: Wind Machines
activities.description: A hands-on workshop where children can learn about wind machines and create their own.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a hands-on creative activity suitable for a 3-year-old's interest in making and building things.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific timings and booking information.

activities.title: Leaders in Community X Young V&A
activities.description: A special event at the Young V&A, likely focused on community engagement and activities for families.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an opportunity to engage in community-focused activities suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific details about the event and its suitability for a 3-year-old.

activities.title: Crafty Kids
activities.description: Little Angel's exciting Saturday morning club for 2-5 year olds and their carers. Members begin the term by watching a performance and meeting the puppets. The following weeks are spent on 'crafty' activities, songs and games linked to the show including some simple puppet making.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for toddlers, this club combines puppet shows with hands-on craft activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk to Dagmar Passage.
transport.tip: Check the Little Angel Theatre website for specific timings and booking information as places are limited.

activities.title: Animal Walk
activities.description: A community-run farm with a variety of animals, including chickens, pigs, goats, and ducks.
activities.whyItWorks: A 3-year-old would enjoy seeing and interacting with farm animals. Offers outdoor space and a chance to learn about animals.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney, easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check the farm's website for any special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: An interactive story centre with indoor and outdoor play areas, workshops, and storytelling sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, hands-on exhibits, and storytelling that will engage a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk to the centre.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Mudlarks
activities.description: An interactive gallery designed for young children, featuring soft play areas, water play, and opportunities to learn about London's history.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a safe and stimulating environment for toddlers with soft play and water play areas. It's also free!

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Canary Wharf, then a short walk to the museum.
transport.tip: Allow plenty of time for exploring the museum, as there are many other exhibits to see.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search high and low for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds. Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the excitement of an egg hunt with the opportunity to see animals. A great way to get exercise and learn.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to Euston, then a bus to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Book zoo tickets in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Follow the trail and match the names of influential characters to the red ribbons adorning the Lindt Gold Bunny statues scattered around the palace grounds, then collect a small chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines history with a fun Easter activity, with a chocolate prize at the end!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book palace tickets in advance and allow plenty of time for exploring the palace and gardens.

activities.title: Easter Quest
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a combination of history, outdoor exploration, and a fun Easter activity. The chocolate reward is a great motivator.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train from London Bridge to Eltham.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific times and booking information.

activities.title: Ham House and Garden Easter Egg Hunt
activities.description: National Trust trail themed around the Civil Wars, with eggs hidden around the gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor fun, egg hunting, and a beautiful setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then a short bus ride or walk to Ham House.
transport.tip: Check the National Trust website for specific details and booking information.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A museum with collections of anthropology, natural history, and musical instruments, plus gardens and an animal walk. The Natural History Gallery is closed until early 2027.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of exhibits and activities suitable for a 3-year-old. The animal walk and gardens provide outdoor space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and temporary exhibitions.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, volcanoes, and fascinating creatures from the natural world. Free activities and family-friendly galleries.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, animals, and interactive exhibits will capture a 3-year-old's imagination. Free admission makes it an affordable day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit early or late to avoid crowds. Book a free timed entry ticket in advance.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits and discover how the world works. The Wonderlab offers hands-on experiments (ticketed).
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and hands-on activities will engage a 3-year-old's curiosity. The Wonderlab provides a fun learning experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book Wonderlab tickets in advance. Check for family-friendly events and demonstrations.

activities.title: Tower Bridge – Easter family activities
activities.description: Discover phenakistoscopes, the original animations that came before cartoons and GIFs. Create your own phenakistoscope of Tower Bridge.
activities.whyItWorks: A creative and historical activity suitable for families.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a bus or walk to Tower Bridge.
transport.tip: Check the Tower Bridge website for specific timings and booking information.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Easter Activities
activities.description: Family-friendly highlights including bumper cars, bungee trampolines, an elephant art trail, a Macaroni Penguin egg hunt, and Colour, Doodle, Draw with Anna Bruder.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Battersea Power Station.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific dates, times and prices of individual activities.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A theatrical production of Alice in Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: The magical story and visual spectacle can captivate young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Check the website for show times and age recommendations.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Every day this Easter a Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a bus tour, afternoon tea, and Peppa Pig, which is popular with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Various starting locations, check website for details.
transport.tip: Book in advance as this is a popular activity.

activities.title: Easter Family Disco
activities.description: Easter Family Disco featuring bubbles, interactive games, prizes and singalongs.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and interactive experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Check Big Penny Social website for location and transport options.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Chelsea Physic Garden Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Follow the trail, search for the eggs and discover the places where the Sprites live. Along the way, complete small tasks and playful challenges to make a special potion to help awaken the Sprites.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Sloane Square, then a short walk to the garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families with shows, musical adventures, puppetry and free activities. Also worth visiting is the REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground – a play space made out of repurposed materials (£7.50).
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities suitable for young children, with both free and paid options.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific events and times.
